trackedSurface.notes: Frontend State coverage is exercised by user-settings-frontend-state-snapshot, user-settings-frontend-state-invalid-fallbacks, and user-settings-frontend-state-tui-namespace; together they verify typed preferences, GUI tab geometry, the TUI namespace, value provenance, invalid-value fallbacks, source-byte preservation, and the non-writing open path. The user-settings-published-defaults case verifies the filesystem-free Rust defaults projection through the public Node snapshot shape. Migration planning coverage verifies current, legacy-flat, and unsupported outcomes, reviewable transition DTOs, exact byte payloads, and the non-writing planning path. Migration reversal coverage verifies the proposed-content revision, swapped endpoints and byte payloads, reversed/inverted change rows, operation after source removal, and an exact involutive round trip. User Settings commit coverage verifies accepted geometry publication, field-specific invalid-dimension rejection, preservation of unknown frontend content, structured stale-revision conflicts, and stable NAPI error codes for operational failures. Explicit bootstrap coverage verifies that ordinary update preview rejects a missing document, bootstrap preview remains read-only, and only bootstrap commit publishes the complete Rust-owned defaults document.

Frontend State coverage is exercised by user-settings-frontend-state-snapshot, user-settings-frontend-state-invalid-fallbacks, and user-settings-frontend-state-tui-namespace; together they verify typed preferences, GUI tab geometry, the TUI namespace, value provenance, invalid-value fallbacks, source-byte preservation, and the non-writing open path. The user-settings-published-defaults case verifies the filesystem-free Rust defaults projection through the public Node snapshot shape. Migration planning coverage verifies current, legacy-flat, and unsupported outcomes, reviewable transition DTOs, exact byte payloads, and the non-writing planning path. Migration reversal coverage verifies the proposed-content revision, swapped endpoints and byte payloads, reversed/inverted change rows, operation after source removal, and an exact involutive round trip. User Settings commit coverage verifies accepted geometry publication, field-specific invalid-dimension rejection, preservation of unknown frontend content, structured stale-revision conflicts, and stable NAPI error codes for operational failures. Explicit bootstrap coverage verifies that ordinary update preview rejects a missing document, bootstrap preview remains read-only, and only bootstrap commit publishes the complete Rust-owned defaults document.
